description: Memory grows without bound (full-file read or unbounded cache) until the kernel OOM-killer terminates the process.
body: Failure mode: the program reads an entire large input into memory or grows a cache/queue without eviction, so resident memory climbs with input size or time until the host/cgroup limit is hit and the OOM-killer terminates the process, sometimes killing a different high-RSS process than the culprit. Detection signals: kernel log shows 'Out of memory: Killed process'; RSS climbs steadily under load; crashes correlate with large inputs. Do not load unbounded data into memory. Safer fallback: stream large inputs in fixed chunks, bound caches and queues with eviction, add backpressure, size against the memory limit, and verify RSS plateaus under sustained load.
